perturbation.original_text: Heritage represents and warrants that: (i) All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications supplied to Heritage by Premier as listed on Schedule A, which Schedule may from time to time be modified by Premier in writing (the "Specifications"), shall conform to Post Holdings' Quality Expectations Manual attached hereto as Schedule E, and shall conform in all material respects to samples previously supplied to Premier by Heritage. No change in Specifications shall be binding on Heritage until Premier has provided written Specifications for each SKU, and each Specification is signed and dated by the Parties. Any additional net cost increases or decreases associated with any modifications to Premier's Specifications shall be borne by or credited to Premier.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage represents and warrants that: (i) All Products manufactured, packaged and delivered by Heritage or Jasper under the terms of this Agreement shall conform to the specifications determined solely by Heritage, ensuring compliance with general industry standards. No specific specifications from Premier are required for production. Any cost increases associated with quality control measures are the sole responsibility of Heritage.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by removing Premier's control over product specifications, which directly conflicts with the original intent of Schedule A and E (Products Processing and Analytical Requirements) and Post Holdings' Quality Expectations Manual. The original text clearly states that products must conform to Premier's Specifications listed on Schedule A and the Post Holdings' Quality Expectations Manual Schedule E. By stating that products should conform to the specifications determined solely by Heritage, and that no specific specifications from Premier are required, Heritage is now self-regulating quality, and not conforming to Premier and Post Holdings' standards. The location where the contradiction occurs is in this section 2(o)(i).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage agrees to perform, at its expense, sampling and testing procedures for the Products in accordance with Schedule B, attached hereto, and all applicable governmental regulations. If additional testing, not identified in Schedule B, is required by Premier, a reasonable additional fee will be agreed upon between Heritage and Premier to cover the associated incremental cost. Other quality control items to be performed under this Agreement are as follows: (i) Normal production runs shall require Premier to provide at least two (2) non-work hour phone numbers for Premier employees who can be contacted in the event a problem occurs during a production run not being conducted during normal business hours. Said contacts and contact information shall be listed in Schedule D. (ii) Heritage shall keep retention samples in accordance with Schedule B. (iii) Heritage shall not modify any processing instructions or Specifications without obtaining Premier's prior written consent. (iv) Heritage shall evaluate Products on a regular schedule at a sufficient frequency to confirm that Products meet the Specifications, including the Post Holdings Quality Expectations Manual. Any Products not conforming to the Specifications shall not be released for shipment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes Premier's control over product specifications, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, which states that Heritage must not modify processing instructions or Specifications without Premier's prior written consent and must evaluate products to confirm they meet the specifications, including the Post Holdings Quality Expectations Manual. The changed text stating that Heritage determines specifications and no specific specifications from Premier are required creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Premier shall have the sole right, exercisable in its discretion, to initiate and direct the content and scope of a recall, market withdrawal, stock recovery, product correction and/or advisory safety communication (any one or more referred to as a "Recall Action") regarding the Products. At Premier's option, Premier can direct Heritage to, and upon such direction Heritage shall, conduct such Recall Action (and Heritage shall ensure Jasper's cooperation). Premier shall determine, in its sole discretion, the manner, text and timing of any publicity to be given such matters upon prior consultation with Heritage. In the event a Recall Action is initiated or directed by Premier, Heritage agrees to fully cooperate and take all such steps as are reasonably requested to implement the Recall Action in a timely and complete manner. Any and all action to be taken in connection with a Recall Action shall be in accordance with FDA policies and other applicable laws and regulations. Heritage shall bear all costs, fees and out-of-pocket expenses associated with any Recall Action which results from (i) Heritage's or Jasper's negligence or willful misconduct, (ii) Heritage's or Jasper's failure to comply with Product Specifications or the Post Holdings Quality Expectations Manual set forth on Schedule A, (iii) any breach of this Agreement by Heritage or (iv) ingredients or packaging materials purchased by Heritage or Jasper. In all other cases, Premier shall bear all costs associated with any Recall Action.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a potential product issue requiring a recall, Heritage shall have the right to manage the recall process, adhering to standard industry practices. Heritage is responsible for all communication regarding the recall.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting Premier's sole right to initiate and direct recall actions and assigning this right to Heritage, the modified text contradicts the original agreement where Premier had full discretion. The phrase 'standard industry practices' introduces ambiguity. This directly conflicts with the original clause about Heritage bearing costs of a recall action if they fail to comply with the specifications and manuals set forth by Premier. The location where the contradiction occurs is in this section 21.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Premier shall indemnify, defend and hold Heritage harmless from and against any and all loss, cost, expense, claim, suit, damage or liability (including reasonable attorneys' fees and court costs) (collectively "Losses") arising out of or relating to an infringement or alleged infringement of any Trademarks or Copyrights in connection with the Products to the extent Heritage follows Premier's instructions with regard to the proper display and use of the Trademarks and Copyrights. In addition, Premier shall indemnify, defend and hold Heritage harmless from and against any and all Losses arising out of or relating to: (i) Heritage's adherence to the Product Specifications, identified in Schedule A, or written orders or instructions given by Premier to Heritage relating to the manufacture or packaging of Products; (ii) Premier's breach of any of its obligations contained herein; and (iii) the storage, sale, marketing, distribution and consumption of the Products, other than any Losses which would be covered under Section 7(b) hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text gives Premier the sole right to manage recalls and assigns cost responsibilities based on the cause of the recall. The changed text gives Heritage the right to manage recalls and removes Premier's control, thereby contradicting the original agreement. This also introduces ambiguity via 'standard industry practices', which could contradict Premier's control over specifications in Schedule A, referenced in the contradicted text, where Premier indemnifies Heritage for following those specifications.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Heritage agrees to handle and store reasonable amounts of raw materials based upon the level of production expected [***]. With regard to finished Products, Heritage agrees during the Term to store finished Products at no cost to Premier for a period not to exceed [***] from the date of Heritage's issuance of a Certificate of Analysis ("COA"). Commencing on [***] after the date the COA is delivered to Premier, a warehouse fee will be imposed that will equal $[***], until such Products are delivered to Premier's carrier. [***] Capability of a corrugated shipping case to withstand double stacking shall be mutually determined and agreed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Heritage agrees to handle and store raw materials and finished products as needed. Storage fees for finished products will be determined by Heritage and billed to Premier monthly.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specifics of the storage agreement, especially the initial free storage period and the subsequent warehouse fee, the modified text introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original terms. Now, the cost and duration of storage are undefined and at the discretion of Heritage, which creates an internal conflict as Premier has no control now, when they originally had it. This contradicts Section 3 (Delivery, Pricing, Billing and Payment), which outlines how Heritage will charge Premier. The location where the contradiction occurs is in this section 4(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Heritage shall charge Premier [***] as set forth in Schedule C.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the previously agreed-upon specifics of storage fees and conditions, giving Heritage complete discretion over billing. This directly contradicts the original contract's intent, as outlined in the provided explanation, which intended to provide Premier with predictable costs. Specifically, the contradicted text states that "Heritage shall charge Premier [***] as set forth in Schedule C." The modified text allows Heritage to determine storage fees independently of Schedule C, thus creating a direct contradiction regarding how and when Premier will be billed. Therefore, a clear contradiction exists.
